---
title: "Week 6: Toolkit & Qualitative Methods"
subtitle: "Chapters 9-10"
date: "2026-09-29"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

## Overview

Chapter 9 introduces the four major research methods. Chapter 10 teaches qualitative methods as standalone approaches: interviews, focus groups, and thematic analysis.

**Reading:** Chapter 9 and Chapter 10

## Key Concepts

- Four methods: content analysis, surveys, experiments, qualitative
- Matching research questions to appropriate methods
- Mixed methods designs
- In-depth interviews: semi-structured design, rapport
- Focus groups: group dynamics as data
- Thematic analysis: Braun and Clarke six-phase process
- Quality criteria: credibility, transferability, dependability, confirmability

## Learning Objectives

1. Describe what each of the four methods can and cannot tell you
2. Distinguish qualitative methods from quantitative content analysis
3. Describe the six phases of thematic analysis
4. Draft a research prospectus aligning Topic, Theory, and Data

## Assignment: Project Prospectus (25 pts)

Write a one-page prospectus: Topic, Theory, Data source, preliminary RQs, and timeline.
